svg.title: Bibb School FY27 Budget: The Range of Options
svg.desc: Bibb County Schools faces a 23.8 million dollar FY27 deficit that the Board must close by Tuesday, June 2. Without action, fund balance falls from 7.8 percent (FY27) to negative 14.3 percent (FY30); emergency loans loom. Every mill not raised must be matched by about 5.4 million dollars of cuts somewhere on the menu. Path A, cuts-heavy: about 1.7 mills (roughly 9 dollars 40 cents per month on a median home) plus every cut on the menu totaling 15.8 million dollars, including bigger class sizes, school closures, outsourcing 80 paraprofessional jobs, a district-wide pay freeze, support-staff furloughs, eliminating unfilled jobs, redistricting, other small cuts, and closing the VIP Academy. Path B, revenue-heavy: about 4.3 mills (roughly 24 dollars per month on a median home) with only about 680 thousand dollars in small operational cuts and no classroom impact. Either path balances the FY27 budget; the difference is who pays.
svg.text: THE GAP TO CLOSE BY TUESDAY, JUNE 2
Every mill not raised must be matched by

svg.text: Sources: FY27 Tentative Adoption (5/14/2026), Budget Work Session No. 3, U.S. Census, Ga. Dept. of Revenue millage data.
* Estimates assume the median Bibb home ($207,100), before any homestead, senior, veteran, or historic exemptions. FY27 rates depend on the 2026 tax digest.
